heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 23, which occurred on 07 September 2025 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 23 December 2025, is ranked 7th among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 9 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 7.8°C), outside temperature (down 4.7°C), and feels like temperature (down 3.8°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 295 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.5°C), outside temperature (up 8.7°C), cloud levels (up 338 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 294.6m lower than yesterday, 337.9m lower than the past week, 24.1m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 828m minimum and 2280m maximum, with an average of 986.3m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1324.2m and 962.2m.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, dry and less humid conditions and winds will be practically absent. Chance of light rain, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and a mix of sun and clouds. High pressure suggests settled weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-2.6°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
